ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Jun-14-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.
Off Calendar Jun-14-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Jun-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-15-298653 The Henry Kai Lui Trust and PES-15-298654 In Re Henry K.H. Lui's Durable Power of Attorney.
Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for (trustee) Henry Kai Hong Lui. Carlos Jato for the respondents.

The Court stated that there are a variety of petitions, that some of the issues have settled and asked where we stand now. Ms. Calhoun stated that she was not present at the settlement conference, that the landlord tenant issues have settled, that they need confirmation on the proper trust instrument, that the settlement agreement will be performed within 6 months and that she has submitted a revised order. Mr. Jato requested that the matters be continued until June to make sure they have time to finalize. The Court stated that Ms. Calhoun is to submit an order limited to confirming that the newer trust is effective and in force and that all other matters are continued for status. The Accounting petition and the petition for instruction, etc., in case PTR-15-298653 and the status of filing an accounting and the petition for order compelling M.Q. Huang as former attorney in fact, etc., were continued to June 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Continued To Sep-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Henry Kai Hong Lui; Dan Berko for Miao Huang
Mr. Berko cofirmed that an accounting has been filed, and provided a courtesy copy of the accounting to the Court at the commencement of the hearing. Ms. Calhoun stated that she had received the accounting on Thursaday and that she has not had time for a thorough review of it, but that there were many problems that she had already found. She stated that it was not in proper format as required by the Probate Code, and that the accompanying declaration of the paralegal contained an admission that over $72,200 of Mr. Lui's money claimed to have been expended on Mr. Lui's account was unaccounted for. Ms. Calhoun stated that Ms. Huang and her husband moved into Mr. Lui's house when he was diagnosed as being terminally ill, temporarily incapacitated, with a projected period of only six months to live. Mr. Lui has survived long past that projection, and in 2014 his doctors determined that his capacity had been restored. Mr. Lui has decided that he wants to sell his house, but Ms. Huang and her husband refused to move out, requiring the filing of eviction proceedings. She further stated that Mr. Lui has no recollection of signing the durable power of attorney granting Ms. Huang the power to act on his behalf and to be added to his bank accounts. Mr. Berko responded that there was no truth to the assertions made by Ms. Calhoun. He stated that there had been an eviction proceeding pending against his clients, but that the eviction complaint was dismissed on the day of trial, and an ejectment action filed instead. He opined that this evidenced the lack of merit in the proceedings against his client. He stated that the accounting goes through every expenditure made on Mr. Lui's accounts, and that it is erroneous to assert that there are $72,000 in unaccounted expenditures. He claimed that he has a number of witnesses to support his clients, including the niece of Mr. Lui who had the power of attorney prepared on Mr. Lui's behalf, with whom Ms. Calhoun has never spoken. He stated that he was not aware a specific format was required for the accounting, but that if the accounting as submitted was not in proper form he would revise it. The Court stated that the hearing needed to be continued to make certain the accounting was in proper form. Mr. Berko stated that there was a mediation scheduled for August 26, 2015, which might produce a global settlement of all disputes. The Court set the following briefing and hearing schedule: Mr. Berko must file and serve any amended accounting by August 20, 2015; Ms. Calhoun must file any opposition by August 28, 2015, and any reply be Mr. Berko is due by September 3, 2015. The hearing on all matters was continued to September 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing An Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Continued To Aug-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Continued To Aug-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order:10 Compelling M.Q. Huang As Forme Attorney-In-Fact Under Springing Power If Attorney For Asset Management To Account And Etc.
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Continued To Jul-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-15-298653 Lui Trust, and PES-15-298654 Lui Durable Power of Attorney.
Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for (petitioner) Henry Lui. Daniel Berko for and with Miao Quiong Huang & Guo Rong Li. Jamie Bombard, counsel for Henry Lui is a general civil matter also present.

Ms. Calhoun stated that she is only suing Mrs. Huang. The Court stated that this is a petition to order an accounting, that a response has been filed and asked if there was anything further. Mr. Berko reported that he has tried to get cooperation from the other party, that they cannot provide an accounting without bank records which they are unable to access, that the other side is not willing to provide copies and that Ms. Huang is not used to these procedures and was not aware of the necessity of keeping better records. Ms. Calhoun stated that it is the duty of the trustee to account and cited various code sections regarding such. Ms. Bombard stated that they have produced hundreds of pages of bank records in the civil action, that Ms. Huang has these records, that Ms. Huang & Mr. Li are the only people who can explain what the withdrawals were for and where the money went. Ms. Calhoun stated that they are requesting a formal accounting, that they had requested Ms. Huang to account in December and that a reply brief was filed on June 9th, providing an endorsed filed copy to the court. Ms. Calhoun further stated that Mr. Lui is able to access his bank records and that there are many cash withdrawals and monies shifted from one account to another that need explanations. Mr. Berko stated that there is zero evidence that Mr. Lui was ever mentally incapacitated and that Ms. Bombard has provided some bank records but not everything. Ms. Bombard stated that they have provided all that they have and that it would at least be a start to the accounting. The court stated that it would grant a continuance so that Ms. Calhoun can provide the necessary records. Ms. Calhoun stated that Mr. Lui is ill, that this is a delay tactic and that they had asked for an accounting in December. Mr. Berko responded that the delay is being caused by counsel not cooperating with the request for records. The Court stated that it will grant the petitions (for both cases) in regards to the request for accountings, that the remaining issues in each petition (for both cases) are under submission and set a status for filing an accounting petition July 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Request To Compel An Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
